label: vulnerable

source.description: User-controlled string from query parameter 'name'. No character filter or length limit is applied.

propagation.description: User input is concatenated directly into the template source string with the '+' operator, embedding it as raw template syntax rather than a context variable.

sink.description: Environment.from_string() parses and compiles the attacker-controlled string as a Jinja2 template. Jinja2 expressions within name (e.g. {{config}}) are evaluated on render().

missing_controls: pass name as a context variable to a static template instead of concatenating
allowlist / regex filter on name before concatenation
use render_template_string with {{ name }} placeholder and name as context kwarg
expected_findings.risk_reasoning: User input is embedded into the template source string before parsing. An attacker supplying '{{config}}' or '{{''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__()}}' can enumerate internal objects and potentially achieve RCE.
expected_findings.remediation_hint: Define a static template string with a {{ name }} placeholder and pass name as a context variable: jinja_env.from_string('Hello, {{ name }}!').render(name=name). Never concatenate user input into template source.

recall_anchor.description: A correct system must identify the sink where user input enters the template engine as raw template syntax.

notes: The Environment is created without autoescape, which is the default. The root cause is string concatenation into template source, not the autoescape setting.
